heroHeadline: Iran Submits Hormuz Reopening Proposal; Trump Warns Tehran to 'Get Smart Soon' as Frozen Conflict Risk Grows
heroSubtitle: Iran submitted a new de-escalation offer on April 28 through Pakistan — proposing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade, while deferring nuclear talks — but the Trump administration expressed deep skepticism, with Trump warning Iran 'better get smart soon' on April 29. Analysts cite a growing 'frozen conflict' risk as both Islamabad rounds have collapsed and the 20-year vs. 5-year enrichment gap remains unresolved. Simultaneously, the UN's Assistant Secretary-General told the Security Council on April 28 that Gaza's ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile,' with 800+ Palestinians killed since the ceasefire began. In a positive development, the DRC National Assembly approved Washington Accords ratification bills on April 28, sending them to the Senate. 10 ceasefire frameworks nominally in force globally.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN, African Union, IGAD, Crisis Group, USIP, PA-X Database, and Tier 1–3 sources across all media poles.
